segments.text: These are the India's most famous brands, but they might be the total copies of these foreign giants. Each one copied a design, but the last one copied
the world's biggest tech company and got away with it. So, the first one is Zudo.
Everyone loved their trendy clothes at cheap prices. But wait, doesn't this design look familiar? It's almost identical to Zara. Zuddio's business
model is literally fast fashion. They take global trends from brands like Zara and H&M and mass produce them for the Indian market at onetenth of the price.
But this copy is small. So, we have Neimann's. They're famous for their revolutionary wool shoes. Super comfortable, eco-friendly, and
sustainable. But look at all birds from the US. Same marino wool material, same distinct no logo look, and same sole design. They did not invent the wool
shoes. They just brought the Allirds concept to India before all birds could.
This is cool. But the boldest one is Firebolt. They became India's number one smartwatch brand once. But look at their gladiator watch. It is exactly like
Apple Watch Ultra. Practically the entire design, the orange button, the crown, take anything. Apple sells their for 80,000 and Firebolt sells the look
for 3,000. Genius or theft? Subscribe and let me know.
